title: The wreck, second time
why.empty: The editor is empty. Deleting is not fixing — that is the first thing to understand here. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed: Something is open and never closed — or closed in the wrong place. Go from top to bottom, tag by tag.
hint2.not-closed: There are two of these. One is a heading. The other is inside the paragraph with the price.
hint3.not-closed:
Whatever you opened last, you close first. If opens INSIDE a

, it has to close inside it too.
hint4.not-closed: Look at the price line. Which tag closes first there, and which one should?
why.fake-list: Somewhere there is something that LOOKS like a list without being one. Remember the lesson with the dashes?
hint2.fake-list: The list starts correctly. Then someone gave up halfway.
hint3.fake-list:
The wheels are inside the
    , but they are not list items — they are bare text with dashes and a
    . The browser shows them because it is forgiving. That does not make them bullets.
hint4.fake-list:
Each of the four things wants its own
  • with a
    • . The dashes and the
    go away — the browser draws the bullets.
why.two-h1: The hierarchy is wrong. One of the headings claims to be about the whole page, and it is not.
hint2.two-h1: Count the top-level headings. How many should a page have?
hint3.two-h1: Which of the two describes the WHOLE page? That one stays. The other is a section inside it.
hint4.two-h1: "What is included" is not the topic of the page — it is a section. Bring it down a level. And do not delete it: it must stay a heading, just a different one.
why.no-quote: Someone else's words are standing as if they were yours. Quotation marks are not a tag.
hint2.no-quote: The last paragraph. Who said it, and where does their part end?
hint3.no-quote: Quotation marks are characters — the computer does not know these words are not yours. There is a tag that says so.
hint4.no-quote: Take the borrowed words out of the sentence, put them in a <blockquote>, and remove the quotation marks. The tag says it now — do not say it twice.
why.lost-content: You deleted something instead of fixing it. The wreck has ALL of its content — the content is not the mistake. The mistake is how it is said. Bring back what you removed and fix it, do not remove it.
hint2.lost-content: Something is missing from: the heading "Bike for sale", the price 18 leva, the front wheel, the rear wheel. Compare with the original code.
blocks.0.text: A wreck again. But this time I am not telling you what you are looking for.
blocks.1.text: The page on the right is broken in several places. Different mistakes, from different lessons. You have learned all of them.
blocks.2.text: I will not tell you how many
blocks.3.text: The first time I told you "four things are missing, all of the same kind". That was a hint. Now there is none.
blocks.4.text: The tests are hidden. They will only tell you that something is wrong — and roughly where to look. They will not point at the line.
blocks.5.text: How to read someone else's code
blocks.6.text: Do not hunt for errors. Read the code and ask of each thing: "does this truthfully say what it IS?". A list that is not a list. A heading that is not the main one. A quote that is only quotation marks.
blocks.7.text: Half the bugs in real code are not syntax. The code works. It just lies.
blocks.8.text: And a warning: deleting is not fixing. Remove the broken thing and the error goes away — but so does the content. Everything on this page must stay. What changes is the way it is said.
